skill.name: azure-search-documents-ts
skill.description: Build search applications using Azure AI Search SDK for JavaScript. Create indexes, implement vector and hybrid search, semantic ranking, and manage search documents.
skill.summary: Use Azure AI Search SDK to build vector search, hybrid search, and semantic ranking solutions.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill contains documentation for Azure AI Search SDK with TypeScript code examples. All static analyzer findings are false positives caused by misinterpreting markdown code fences. The skill reads environment variables for configuration, which is standard practice for Azure SDK authentication.

content.value_statement: Implement powerful search capabilities with Azure AI Search SDK. Create vector indexes, hybrid search, and semantic ranking for intelligent document retrieval.
actual_capabilities: Create and manage Azure Search indexes with vector field configurations
Upload, merge, and delete documents in batch operations
Perform full-text search with filtering and faceting
Implement vector similarity search using embeddings
Combine text and vector search for hybrid results
Enable semantic ranking and extractive answers
limitations: Requires Azure Search service subscription and endpoint configuration
Vector embeddings must be generated by external embedding service
Semantic search requires Azure Search tier that supports it
Authentication requires Azure credentials or API keys

use_cases.title: Product Search with Semantic Ranking
use_cases.description: Build an e-commerce search that combines text matching with vector similarity for relevant product discovery.

use_cases.title: Document Retrieval System
use_cases.description: Create a knowledge base search with hybrid search for finding relevant documents by meaning and keywords.

use_cases.title: Autocomplete and Suggestions
use_cases.description: Implement search-as-you-type with Azure Search suggesters for improved user experience.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Index Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Azure Search index for a products catalog with fields for id, title, description, category, and price. Make title and description searchable.

prompt_templates.title: Vector Search Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Add vector search to my Azure Search index. I have 1536-dimensional embeddings from Azure OpenAI. Show me how to configure the vector field and perform similarity search.

prompt_templates.title: Hybrid Search Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement hybrid search that combines keyword matching with vector similarity. Show me how to tune the results and rank by relevance.

prompt_templates.title: Semantic Search with Answers
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure semantic search on my Azure Search index to return extractive answers and captions. Explain the semantic configuration and query options needed.

output_examples.input: Search for products matching 'wireless headphones' with category filter
output_examples.output: Returns ranked product results with relevance scores, filtered by Electronics category, including title, price, and description snippets

output_examples.input: Vector search for documents similar to a query embedding
output_examples.output: Returns semantically similar documents ranked by vector distance, even without exact keyword matches

best_practices: Use hybrid search combining text and vector for best relevance across query types
Enable semantic ranking to improve results for natural language queries
Batch document operations using uploadDocuments instead of single document calls
anti_patterns: Do not upload documents one at a time - use batch operations for efficiency
Avoid using includeTotalCount in production queries as it impacts performance
Do not skip index field configuration - properly mark searchable, filterable, and facetable fields

faq.question: What Azure Search tier do I need for vector search?
faq.answer: Vector search requires Azure Search Basic tier or higher. Semantic search requires Standard tier or higher.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure Search?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production with managed identity, or admin/query API keys for development. Store credentials in environment variables.

faq.question: What embedding models work with Azure Search vector fields?
faq.answer: Any embedding model that produces numeric vectors. Azure OpenAI embeddings (text-embedding-ada-002) produce 1536-dimensional vectors commonly used with Azure Search.

faq.question: How do I update existing documents in the index?
faq.answer: Use mergeOrUploadDocuments with the document key. Azure Search merges fields you provide and preserves existing values for unspecified fields.

faq.question: Can I filter vector search results?
faq.answer: Yes, combine vectorSearchOptions with filter parameter. Filtering happens before vector search, reducing the candidate set for similarity matching.

faq.question: What is the difference between semantic and vector search?
faq.answer: Vector search finds similar content using embeddings. Semantic search uses AI to understand query intent, extract answers, and provide captions. They can be used together for best results.
